name: Sanchez, Louie
resolved_employer: Hance Scarborough, LLP — Legislative Consultant (contract lobbyist)
evidence: Louie M. Sanchez is the Legislative Consultant at Hance Scarborough LLP in Austin, an exact match to the filed employer and the filed occupation 'Consultant'. He joined the firm in December 2020/2021 after roughly a decade as Chief of Staff to Sen. Eddie Lucio Jr. (D-Brownsville), and the firm bio says he represents trade associations, local governments, and industry clients in aerospace, energy, health policy, private equity, and technology. A Capitol lobbyist giving to Kirk Watson in Oct 2022 fits the pattern; no FEC federal giving history.

affiliations.role: Legislative Consultant; his own firm bio describes a client portfolio that includes 'industry leaders in aerospace, energy, health policy, private equity, and technology,' and credits him with work on Texas space launch authorization and spaceport infrastructure funding. Specific registered clients were not verified against Texas Ethics Commission filings, and the cited aerospace work is commercial spaceflight rather than defense contracting, so this is recorded as a lobbying portfolio and not as an oil/gas or military-defense affiliation.

name: Cabrera, Cynthia
resolved_employer: Hometown Hero (Austin-based hemp-derived THC/CBD products company) — Chief Strategy Officer
evidence: Cynthia Cabrera was appointed Chief Strategy Officer of Hometown Hero CBD in June 2022, matching the filed employer 'Hometown Heros' and the filed occupation 'CSO' exactly. Hometown Hero is headquartered in Austin and she lives in Buda (78610, Hays County, Austin metro), consistent with a 2024 gift to Austin Mayor Kirk Watson. She is also founder/president of the Texas Hemp Business Council and previously ran the Cating Group, a hemp/medical-marijuana consultancy. Classified Retail as the closest taxonomy fit for a direct-to-consumer hemp products brand; note her heavy federal giving history (48 FEC donations, strongly Republican-leaning lean score of 0.07) is consistent with an industry lobbying posture toward the party controlling Texas.

name: Norris, Genie
resolved_employer: ColorGenie Color Consulting — owner and creative director (architectural color consultant)
evidence: Genie Norris is the owner/creative director of ColorGenie Color Consulting, an architectural color consulting practice she opened in Austin in 2009. Business listings place ColorGenie in Austin 78746, an exact match to the donor zip, and the filed occupation 'Color Consultant' and employer 'ColorGenie' both match. She has been featured in HGTV, Domino, Tribeza, and Austin Home. Her 22 FEC donations lean fully Democratic (lean 1.0), consistent with a gift to Kirk Watson. Classified Architecture as the closest taxonomy fit for architectural/interior color consulting; Yelp categorizes the business under Interior Design.

name: Ferrier, Julian
resolved_employer: Double Line, Inc. (Austin data and technology strategy consultancy serving state/local government and nonprofits)
evidence: The filed employer resolves cleanly: Double Line, Inc. is an Austin firm founded in 2009 that provides data and technology strategy services to state and local government and nonprofit clients in K-12 education, health, and justice, and was a 2022 Inc. 5000 honoree. The individual could not be confirmed — no LinkedIn or other profile for a Julian Ferrier at Double Line in Austin surfaced (searches returned a UK estate agent of the same name). The industry is assigned from the verified employer rather than from the person, and the filed occupation 'Business Analyst' is consistent with Double Line's line of work, so confidence is medium.

name: O'Hara, Andrew
resolved_employer: Modern Pediatrics (Austin concierge pediatric primary care) — co-founder and CEO
evidence: Andrew O'Hara is co-founder and CEO of Modern Pediatrics, based in Austin, matching the filed employer and the filed occupation 'CEO' exactly. He previously founded and led Chiron Health, an Austin telemedicine platform, and holds a master's in medical informatics from Northwestern. Austin 78704 residence and 14 FEC donations that lean fully Democratic (lean 1.0) are consistent with the 2022 gift to Kirk Watson.

name: Broaddus, Linda Sue
resolved_employer: Broaddus Enterprises, Inc. — bookkeeping; the company is part of the Broaddus family petroleum business (Broaddus Petroleum Corporation), a Central Texas fuel distributor and Chevron/Texaco station operator
evidence: Broaddus Petroleum's own site identifies Scott W. 'Bill' Broaddus Jr. as president of Broaddus Petroleum Corporation, Broaddus Enterprises, Inc., and Broaddus Properties G.P., and names his wife Linda; public records place Linda S Broaddus (age ~71) on Lime Creek Rd in Volente, an exact match to the filed location of Volente, TX 78641. The filed occupation 'Bookkeeping' at Broaddus Enterprises matches the company's stated function of handling retail store operations and accounting for the family's fuel business. Industry is a judgment call between fuel distribution and convenience-store retail — Bloomberg profiles Broaddus Enterprises itself as a supplier of fuel, lubricants, LPG, and petrochemical products, so Energy / Environment was chosen.

affiliations.org: Broaddus Petroleum Corporation / Broaddus Enterprises, Inc.
affiliations.role: Bookkeeper at the family-owned Central Texas petroleum distribution and Chevron/Texaco branded station business; wife of president/owner Scott W. 'Bill' Broaddus Jr.

name: Ripple, Christopher
resolved_employer: Bento Box Entertainment (Los Angeles animation studio — 'Bob's Burgers', 'The Great North') — Supervisor, Digital Production
evidence: Christopher Ripple is Supervisor of Digital Production at Bento Box Entertainment in the Los Angeles area, matching the filed employer 'Bento box entertainment' and the filed occupation 'Production supervisor'. He rose from production assistant to digital production coordinator on 'Bob's Burgers' and 'The Great North'. The filed Glendale, CA 91202 address sits in Bento Box's LA base, and his 4 FEC donations lean fully Democratic (lean 1.0), consistent with an out-of-state 2024 gift to Travis County Commissioner candidate Krista Laine — presumably a personal rather than industry connection.

name: Taylor, Kathy

name: Lyon, Benjamin
resolved_employer: C.C. Carlton Industries, Ltd. (Austin-area commercial/residential site and civil construction contractor) — Chief Executive Officer
evidence: Benjamin Lyon is CEO of C.C. Carlton Industries, Ltd., matching the filed employer exactly and the filed occupation 'Executive'. He rose through the firm as Estimator, Director of Estimating, and VP of Estimating, and holds a Texas A&M degree. The company is Austin-based and the donor's Leander 78641 residence is in its immediate service area, consistent with the 2024 gift to Kirk Watson. No FEC federal giving history.

name: Ali, Mehdi
evidence: Could not confirm. No entity named 'Escarpment Enterprises Inc' could be located in Texas records or anywhere on the web (the similarly named Escarpment Construction LLC of Austin is run by Warren Spain, not an Ali). A plausible but unproven lead: a Mehdi Ali in Austin is president of the Greater Austin Merchants Cooperative Association (GAMA), a 600-member convenience-store and gas-station buying cooperative, and holds an accounting/finance degree from Karachi University — an Austin 'Businessman' consistent with the filed occupation, and 'Escarpment' would be a natural entity name for a store-holding company given Escarpment Blvd in southwest Austin. But nothing links him to the filed employer or to the donor's 78738 (Bee Cave/Lakeway) ZIP, so no industry is assigned.
